steps.title: Identify the decisive requirement
steps.content: The goal states “specify WHO may have first populated the Azores, according to the 2015 study” — the answer must directly name the specific group the study identified.

steps.title: Find the choice that directly names the group
steps.content: Choice D directly identifies “Vikings from Scandinavia” and attributes this to “a 2015 study,” fully answering the WHO question while contrasting with the previous Portuguese theory. Choices A, B, and C fail to do this: A emphasizes the old historical belief, B only negates Portuguese involvement without naming Vikings, and C focuses on the genetic mouse evidence rather than directly identifying who populated the islands. This matches choice D — D is correct.
